rationale: The citing paper gives this source a specific methodological role rather than just listing it in background references. In its guidelines discussion, it uses Jap and Ganesan (2000) as an explicit example of how a cross-sectional design can still study relationship dynamics by classifying life-cycle stages, and it explains that logic in detail. This directly supports the citing paper's argument about when longitudinal data are and are not necessary.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Jap and Ganesan (2000) as illustrative evidence that cross-sectional surveys can model relationship life-cycle effects without tracking the same firms over time. This example is used to support the paper's methodological positioning about the conditions under which cross-sectional designs remain valid.
